evals.prompt: I want to add BarcodeCapture to my Flutter app. Here's my starter page: EmptyPage.dart. I need to scan EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes for a retail app.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, writes complete BarcodeCapture integration code into EmptyPage.dart (BLoC + page widget), and shows the setup checklist (pub packages to add, flutter pub get, iOS Info.plist, Android runtime permission, Android minSdk).

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown (mentions scandit_flutter_datacapture_barcode and flutter pub get)
Setup checklist mentions iOS NSCameraUsageDescription in ios/Runner/Info.plist
Setup checklist mentions Android runtime camera permission (via permission_handler)
await ScanditFlutterDataCaptureBarcode.initialize() is present in main() before runApp
WidgetsFlutterBinding.ensureInitialized() is called before the await
DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( or DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present
A license key placeholder string is present (semantic)
BarcodeCapture(settings) is used (v8 constructor form, not BarcodeCapture.forContext)
dataCaptureContext.addMode( or dataCaptureContext.setMode( is called for the BarcodeCapture instance
Symbology.ean13Upca is enabled
Symbology.code128 is enabled
No symbologies except ean13Upca and code128 are enabled
Camera.defaultCamera is used as the frame source
BarcodeCapture.createRecommendedCameraSettings() is applied to the camera
DataCaptureView.forContext( is used to create the view
BarcodeCaptureOverlay(barcodeCapture) is constructed and attached with captureView.addOverlay(overlay)
barcodeCapture.addListener( is called to register the scan listener
A class implements BarcodeCaptureListener with didScan and didUpdateSession overrides
didScan signature uses Future<FrameData?> Function() for the getFrameData parameter
Imports come from scandit_flutter_datacapture_core (DataCaptureContext, Camera, DataCaptureView) and scandit_flutter_datacapture_barcode (BarcodeCaptureSettings, Symbology)
package:scandit_flutter_datacapture_barcode/scandit_flutter_datacapture_barcode_capture.dart is imported for BarcodeCapture, BarcodeCaptureOverlay, and BarcodeCaptureListener

evals.prompt: Add BarcodeCapture barcode scanning to ProductListBloc.dart. When a barcode is scanned, add it to the scannedItems list and emit through the existing stream.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds BarcodeCapture to ProductListBloc.dart alongside the existing stream/state code. All existing stream controller, list, and clear logic are preserved.

assertions.text: The BLoC implements BarcodeCaptureListener
DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( or DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present
BarcodeCapture(settings) is used
dataCaptureContext.addMode( or dataCaptureContext.setMode( is called
DataCaptureView.forContext( or reference to it is present (the BLoC exposes captureView/context for the page to mount)
BarcodeCaptureOverlay is created and attached to the view
barcodeCapture.addListener(this) is called
didScan override is present
didScan appends to the existing scannedItems list
Existing scannedItems list is preserved
Existing stream controller (_scannedItemsController) is preserved and emits on new scans
Existing clear() method is preserved

evals.prompt: When a barcode is scanned in my Flutter app, I want to disable scanning, look up the code in a database (a long-running async call), and re-enable scanning when the lookup completes. Wire this up safely.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets barcodeCapture.isEnabled = false at the start of didScan, awaits the lookup, then sets isEnabled = true again. Avoids blocking the recognition pipeline by not doing the heavy work synchronously inside the callback.

assertions.text: barcodeCapture.isEnabled = false is set inside didScan
An async lookup or Future is awaited inside didScan (or scheduled outside) before re-enabling
barcodeCapture.isEnabled = true is set after the lookup completes
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, captureView, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to suppress the default beep and vibration when a barcode is scanned in my Flutter app — silent scanning. Update the BarcodeCapture feedback.
evals.expected_output: The skill assigns a Feedback with null sound and null vibration to barcodeCapture.feedback.success, or assigns a new BarcodeCaptureFeedback() (empty constructor) — both forms suppress feedback.

assertions.text: barcodeCapture.feedback.success is assigned a Feedback(null, null), or a new BarcodeCaptureFeedback() with empty constructor is assigned to barcodeCapture.feedback (semantic)

In the recommended/primary feedback configuration, both Sound.defaultSound and Vibration.defaultVibration are removed from the success Feedback (alternative or commented-out examples may still mention them) (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, captureView, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Add a square viewfinder to my BarcodeCapture preview so users can see where to aim. Use the standard Scandit RectangularViewfinder.
evals.expected_output: The skill creates a RectangularViewfinder and assigns it to overlay.viewfinder. The overlay is the BarcodeCaptureOverlay that was created earlier.

assertions.text: A RectangularViewfinder (or similar Viewfinder) is constructed
The viewfinder is assigned to a BarcodeCaptureOverlay's viewfinder property (overlay.viewfinder = ...)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, captureView, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need to filter scans so duplicates of the same code within 500 milliseconds are ignored. Update my BarcodeCapture settings.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets codeDuplicateFilter on the BarcodeCaptureSettings instance (as a Duration of 500 ms) before constructing the BarcodeCapture mode.

assertions.text: settings.codeDuplicateFilter is set to Duration(milliseconds: 500)
codeDuplicateFilter is set BEFORE BarcodeCapture(settings) is constructed
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, captureView, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need to handle the camera lifecycle properly — when the app goes to background the camera should turn off, and when it resumes the camera should turn back on. Wire this up.
evals.expected_output: The skill uses WidgetsBindingObserver and didChangeAppLifecycleState to call camera.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.on) on resumed and switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.off) on paused/inactive.

assertions.text: WidgetsBindingObserver is mixed into the page State
didChangeAppLifecycleState is overridden
camera.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.on) is called when the lifecycle state is resumed
camera.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.off) is called for paused / inactive (or any non-resumed state)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, captureView, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need to clean up properly when my Flutter page is disposed — remove the BarcodeCapture listener, close the scan stream, and turn off the camera so resources are released.
evals.expected_output: The skill wires barcodeCapture.removeListener, StreamController.close(), and camera.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.off) into the BLoC/state dispose() chain.

assertions.text: A dispose() method exists on the BLoC / controller
barcodeCapture.removeListener(this) is called inside dispose()
The StreamController (scan events) is closed inside dispose()
camera.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.off) is called from State.dispose() or BLoC.dispose()
The page State.dispose() invokes the BLoC's dispose()
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, captureView, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to restrict scanning to the centre 90% by 30% of the camera preview using a rectangular location selection. Update my BarcodeCapture settings.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets settings.locationSelection to a RectangularLocationSelection sized 0.9 x 0.3 in MeasureUnit.fraction.

assertions.text: settings.locationSelection is assigned a RectangularLocationSelection (or equivalent)
MeasureUnit.fraction is used for the size with width 0.9 and height 0.3
locationSelection is set BEFORE BarcodeCapture(settings) is constructed
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, captureView, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Switch BarcodeCapture to manual scan intention — I'm using single-image frames in my Flutter app and Smart mode doesn't work for that.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets settings.scanIntention = ScanIntention.manual on the BarcodeCaptureSettings instance before constructing the BarcodeCapture mode.

assertions.text: settings.scanIntention = ScanIntention.manual is set
scanIntention is set BEFORE BarcodeCapture(settings) is constructed
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, captureView, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Add an aimer viewfinder to my BarcodeCapture preview so users have a dot to aim at the barcode. Use the built-in Scandit aimer.
evals.expected_output: The skill constructs an AimerViewfinder and assigns it to the BarcodeCaptureOverlay's viewfinder property. The overlay is promoted to a field so it can be mutated after construction.

assertions.text: AimerViewfinder() is constructed
The viewfinder is assigned to the overlay via overlay.viewfinder =
RectangularViewfinder is NOT used (the user asked for the aimer)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, captureView, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want a horizontal laser-line viewfinder on my BarcodeCapture preview instead of a rectangle. Use the standard Scandit laserline.
evals.expected_output: The skill constructs a LaserlineViewfinder and assigns it to the BarcodeCaptureOverlay's viewfinder property. The overlay is promoted to a field so it can be mutated after construction.

assertions.text: LaserlineViewfinder() is constructed
The viewfinder is assigned to the overlay via overlay.viewfinder =
RectangularViewfinder is NOT used (the user asked for the laserline)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, captureView, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Change the highlight drawn over a recognized barcode in my BarcodeCapture preview to a green fill with a green outline. Set a custom overlay brush.
evals.expected_output: The skill assigns a Brush(fillColor, strokeColor, strokeWidth) to the BarcodeCaptureOverlay's brush property, using a green fill and green stroke. The overlay is promoted to a field so the brush can be set after construction.

assertions.text: A Brush is constructed with the (fillColor, strokeColor, strokeWidth) positional form
The brush is assigned to the overlay via overlay.brush =
The overlay is held as a field (promoted from the throwaway constructor local) so brush can be assigned
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, captureView, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my BarcodeCapture app I only want to accept barcodes whose data starts with 'PROD-'. Any other code should be ignored and not highlighted. Wire up this rejection logic.
evals.expected_output: Inside didScan the skill checks barcode.data against the 'PROD-' prefix; for a non-matching code it sets overlay.brush = Brush.transparent and returns early without recording the scan. Matching codes are processed as before. The overlay is promoted to a field.

assertions.text: barcode.data is inspected inside didScan to decide accept vs reject
The accept condition checks the 'PROD-' prefix (startsWith or equivalent) (semantic)
For a rejected code, overlay.brush = Brush.transparent is set
For a rejected code, the callback returns early without adding the barcode to the scanned list
The overlay is held as a field so its brush can be mutated
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, captureView, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I'm scanning Code 39 barcodes that use the Full ASCII character set. Enable the Full ASCII extension on Code 39 in my BarcodeCapture settings.
evals.expected_output: The skill gets the Code 39 SymbologySettings via settings.settingsForSymbology(Symbology.code39) and enables the extension with setExtensionEnabled('full_ascii', enabled: true), before the BarcodeCapture mode is constructed.

assertions.text: settings.settingsForSymbology(Symbology.code39) is called
setExtensionEnabled('full_ascii', enabled: true) is called on the Code 39 SymbologySettings
The extension is enabled BEFORE BarcodeCapture(settings) is constructed
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, captureView, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: My Code 39 barcodes use a mod-43 checksum. Configure the optional checksum for Code 39 in my BarcodeCapture settings.
evals.expected_output: The skill gets the Code 39 SymbologySettings and sets checksums to a Set containing Checksum.mod43, before the BarcodeCapture mode is constructed.

assertions.text: settings.settingsForSymbology(Symbology.code39) is called
checksums is assigned a Set containing Checksum.mod43
The checksum is set BEFORE BarcodeCapture(settings) is constructed
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, captureView, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: My Code 128 codes vary in length from 7 to 20 characters and some aren't being read. Widen the active symbol counts for Code 128 in my BarcodeCapture settings.
evals.expected_output: The skill gets the Code 128 SymbologySettings and assigns activeSymbolCounts a Set of integers covering 7 through 20, before the BarcodeCapture mode is constructed.

assertions.text: settings.settingsForSymbology(Symbology.code128) is called
activeSymbolCounts is assigned a Set of ints covering 7 through 20
activeSymbolCounts is set BEFORE BarcodeCapture(settings) is constructed
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, captureView, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Some of our Code 128 labels are printed light-on-dark (color inverted). Enable reading of color-inverted Code 128 codes in my BarcodeCapture settings.
evals.expected_output: The skill gets the Code 128 SymbologySettings and sets isColorInvertedEnabled = true, before the BarcodeCapture mode is constructed.

assertions.text: settings.settingsForSymbology(Symbology.code128) is called
isColorInvertedEnabled = true is set on the Code 128 SymbologySettings
isColorInvertedEnabled is set BEFORE BarcodeCapture(settings) is constructed
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, captureView, listener) is preserved
